div.p: Herbert, Chamberlain of the king of Scots, to all sons of Catholic Mother Church, Greeting: Be it known to you that I have granted and by my charter made firm to the church of Holyrood at Maidens’ Castle and to the canons serving God there the church of Kinneil with all its rights, in teinds, in lands, in wood and plain, in pastures and meadows, in perpetual alms, by the consent and counsel of my sons Stephen and William. I therefore will that the aforesaid canons possess the aforenamed church as freely and quietly as any church in the whole of Lothian is freely and quietly possessed. Reserving the tenure of William my nephew, for as long as he should wish to hold the aforenamed church. As witnesses ... Hugh Giffard, Terry my nephew, and Peter, and Richard the clerk. At Kinneil.
